summary: Comprehensive end-to-end testing of Node.js + Express + EJS + MariaDB POS system. Backend pytest suite (21/21 passing) covering auth, RBAC, products, categories, customers, inventory, sales, suppliers, branches, dashboard, settings, reports, and web pages. Frontend Playwright suite covering login page, RTL language switch, admin login, dashboard KPIs, full sidebar nav, POS terminal with add-to-cart and payment flow, all module pages, logout and cashier role-based hiding of restricted nav items.

issues: X-axis label shows full ISO timestamp '06-22T00:00:00.000Z' instead of a friendly date like '2026-06-22'. Should format day labels.

action_items: (Done by tester) Fixed /app/backend/middleware/auth.js requireAuth and requireRole - they checked req.path which excludes the /api mount prefix, causing /api/* unauthenticated calls to 302-redirect to /login (instead of 401) and authenticated-but-forbidden /api/* calls to crash trying to render error.ejs (returning 500 instead of 403). Replaced with helper using req.originalUrl/req.baseUrl. All 21 backend tests pass after fix.
(Cosmetic) Format the dashboard 7-day revenue chart x-axis labels to drop the ISO timestamp and show only YYYY-MM-DD or short weekday.
critical_code_review_comments: middleware/auth.js: original `req.path.startsWith('/api/')` check was broken because Express strips the mount path inside middleware. Fixed during this run by using req.originalUrl.
middleware/auth.js: setAuthCookie uses secure:false hardcoded. Since the app is served behind HTTPS preview, set secure:true (or based on NODE_ENV==='production'/req.secure) to be safer.

routes/auth.js: API login endpoint /api/auth/login is NOT behind the /api requireAuth (it lives on the root router), good. However bcrypt.compare gets `password || ''` so very short empty passwords are processed - minor.
routes/api/users.js: catch blocks return raw e.message which leaks SQL error details; replace with generic 'server_error'.
routes/api/products.js & similar: pagination/limit not enforced - could lead to large payloads at scale.
Cookie 'access_token' has sameSite:'lax' and secure:false which works behind the preview proxy; ensure secure:true in real production deployment.
seed_data_creation: Backend test created 2 TEST_Product entries (SKU prefix TEST_SKU_*) via POST /api/products which also auto-created inventory rows across 3 branches (so inventory now shows ~48 rows instead of original 42, products grid now 16 instead of 14). These TEST_ records are still in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite is at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Admin login via Bearer token works. The middleware fix in /app/backend/middleware/auth.js is required for any future API auth tests to behave correctly (401/403 instead of redirects). Two leftover TEST_ products in DB - main agent may want to add a cleanup teardown if running repeatedly. Dashboard endpoint is /api/dashboard/summary (not /api/dashboard).
rca of the issue: RCA for the 2 backend auth bugs fixed during testing: Express strips the mount path from req.path inside mounted middleware. Because requireAuth/requireRole are mounted via app.use('/api', ...), `req.path` is '/products', not '/api/products', so `req.path.startsWith('/api/')` was always false. Result: unauthenticated /api/* calls fell through to a 302 redirect to /login; forbidden /api/* calls tried to render error.ejs and crashed with 500. Fix: use req.originalUrl (or req.baseUrl) which preserves the full URL. Reproduce: GET /api/products without cookie -> got 302 -> after fix -> 401. cashier GET /api/users -> got 500 -> after fix -> 403.
